description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture, specled.triangulation, specled.spec_review, specled.decision_governance

.spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md rewrite: aggregate-first intent; new must-requirements aggregate_ingest, versioned_artifact (supersedes the no-in-band-version clause), formatter_disarmed_by_default, line_level_delta_snapshots (per-test lane), cast_time_compaction, degenerate_artifact_rejected; serialized_run rescoped to --per-test and escalated warning->failure; keyed_by_test_pid rewritten to honest {module, name} key (pid claim confirmed fictional); new scenarios: never-executed function ABSENT from records; bare wiring produces no artifact. .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md: restate untested_realization/execution_reach_metric over the envelope; gate untethered_test/underspecified_realization on mode :per_test; new closure_coverage_metric requirement; Decision 1: fix the cold_run_detector_unavailable scenario's false 'mix spec.check runs triangulation' premise — rescope to spec.triangle/spec.review, spec.check stays fast, NO gate wiring. .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md: coverage_tab_bind_closure restated; degraded states. NEW ADR specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md superseding-in-part serialized_per_test_coverage (serialization rationale retained verbatim for the opt-in lane; the &:cover.analyse/1 default and default-posture claims superseded; codifies headline-ANY-test + self-verified semantics, read-only/never-reset invariant, OTP recommend->=27.2-no-hard-gate matrix, versioned envelope). mix spec.check must pass for all touched subjects.

## Orchestrator addenda (admission, epic @ 3abf5e1)

REALITY CHECK: much of this ticket's originally-planned spec surgery already landed as incremental co-changes (155.1-155.7): store_v2_* requirements, aggregate_ingest, serialized_run rescoped to --per-test, formatter arming/disarm requirements, envelope triangulation requirements, coverage_tab_bind_closure v2 rewrite. Your job is the CONSOLIDATION and the durable ADR, not re-doing landed work:

1. NEW ADR .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md — the centerpiece. Supersedes-in-part serialized_per_test_coverage (retain its serialization rationale verbatim for the opt-in lane; supersede the &:cover.analyse/1 default and default-posture claims). Codify: aggregate-first default; headline metric = %% of realization-closure MFAs covered by ANY test; self-verified composite verdict (never a fake per-spec percent); versioned v2 envelope (supersedes any no-in-band-version clause); read-only/never-:cover.reset one-owner invariant (empirically verified); OTP posture per maintainer decision 4 (recommend >= 27.2, no hard gate); legacy artifacts rejected per decision 5 (no auto-migrate); task name kept per decision 2; consumers = spec.triangle + spec.review only per decision 1.
2. AUDIT-CARRIED OBLIGATION (from 155.5 audit, binding): the renderer labels a non-degraded :per_test envelope 'exact per-test attribution', but the engine cannot detect the ExUnit async-cast race (specled_-cpw; ~1/3 exclusive-attribution failures measured; disclosed in Formatter moduledoc + ADR amendment). Reconcile ALL claim surfaces to honest language (approximate/observed attribution, race-bounded): the html.ex/spec.triangle.ex label strings, spec_review.spec.md / triangulation.spec.md / coverage_capture.spec.md statements, and the new ADR. Reference specled_-cpw where the bound is described.
3. Coherence pass over coverage_capture.spec.md / triangulation.spec.md / spec_review.spec.md: retire stale claims the incremental co-changes missed — specifically hunt (a) any keyed_by_test_pid/test_pid-keying claim (root-cause report called the pid claim fictional; verify against current formatter code and fix or confirm already-fixed), (b) any remaining text implying mix spec.check runs triangulation (decision 1: it does not and will not — the cold_run_detector_unavailable scenario premise), (c) scenarios promised by the design that may be missing: never-executed function ABSENT from records; bare test_helper wiring produces NO artifact.
4. Do NOT weaken any requirement that landed with passing verification. Append-only discipline for ADRs (adr_append_only). All spec.check targets for every touched subject must pass.

Spec co-change authorization applies as usual; .spec/realization_hashes.json is tool-managed. A green gate outranks Out of Scope.

LESSONS:
- append new code below existing api_boundary-bound functions (specled_-wp7, from 155.1)
- mix compile --force before spec.check seeds hashes for brand-new bound functions (from 155.6)

## Allowed touches

- .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md, .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md (new)
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage.md (append-only amendment only, if supersession notice is required there)
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex (ONLY label/wording strings for obligation 2 + the test assertions those strings pin)
- test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_triangle_test.exs, test/specled_ex/review_test.exs (only assertions pinned to reworded labels)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-regenerated)

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/specled_ex/coverage/** and all other lib/** beyond the two label-string files
- test/** beyond the three listed label-assertion files
- docs/** (T9)
- priv/**

## Out of Scope (intent)

- No behavior changes — wording/spec/ADR only
- No requirement weakening beyond honest-language reconciliation (obligation 2)
- Nothing wired into mix spec.check

## Verification

- New ADR exists, passes decision-governance/doc-identifier lint, referenced from the specs it governs
- adr_append_only detector silent across all decision-file changes
- No claim surface (spec statement, moduledoc reference, html label, task output) still says 'exact' per-test attribution; race-bounded language references specled_-cpw
- grep proves no spec text claims spec.check runs triangulation
- keyed_by_test_pid claim verified against current formatter code (fixed or confirmed-fixed)
- Scenarios present: never-executed function absent from records; bare wiring produces no artifact
- Full mix spec.check green (all touched subjects, command execution on); doc-identifier lint green
- Label-pinned tests updated and green; mix compile --warnings-as-errors, format, deps.unlock green

This is the precise phrase obligation 2 exists to eliminate, it directly contradicts the new ADR shipped in this same commit (aggregate_first_spec_coverage lines 210-214: every surface describing :ok_per_test data must say observed/approximate, never exact), and it is doubly false today because that mode's per_test clause (same file, 533-539) is still a file-level proxy and the cpw race bounds attribution regardless (jjq does not fix the "exact" wording). Verification bullet "No claim surface (spec statement, moduledoc reference, html label, task output) still says 'exact' per-test attribution" is therefore unmet. Pre-existing + untouched, but the IMPLEMENTED comment asserts obligation-2 completion (and affirmatively audited spec.triangle.ex for "exact") without disclosing this survivor.
- Secondary (advisory-weight): test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs:219 test name asserts "(exact attribution is per-test only)" — same stale overclaim, also pre-existing/untouched/Out-of-Scope.

Notes: Both survivors live in Out-of-Scope files (lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex, test/**), so the fix needs an orchestrator scope decision: either widen Allowed touches for a one-line honest-language @doc edit (+ test-name), or fold the @doc reconciliation into specled_-jjq explicitly. Everything else in the diff verifies clean; this is a narrow honest-language completeness gap, not a behavior or requirement-weakening defect.

AUDIT: BOUNCE → implementer

Session: audit-1
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.8/codex-audit-1/log

Reasoning: FAIL ratified. coverage_closure.ex:317 is a genuine public claim surface — the build_v2/2 @doc says "renderers can distinguish exact per-test attribution (`:ok_per_test`)", the precise phrase obligation 2 exists to eliminate, and it directly contradicts the ADR shipped in the same commit (specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage lines 210-214: every :ok_per_test-describing surface must say observed/approximate, never exact, referencing specled_-cpw). It is doubly false — the :per_test clause at coverage_closure.ex:533-539 is still a file-level proxy (specled_-jjq) and the specled_-cpw race bounds attribution regardless. The test NAME at coverage_closure_test.exs:219 ("exact attribution is per-test only") is the same stale overclaim. A full-corpus 'exact'+per-test sweep confirms these are the ONLY two survivors (html.ex:3002 "observed, not proven exact" and ADR:188/257 are correct honest language). The verifier did NOT misapply obligation 2 — its wording is "Reconcile ALL claim surfaces" (emphatic ALL; the enumerated file list is illustrative) and the verification bullet "No claim surface … still says 'exact' per-test attribution" is broad and unmet; the enumerated Gap therefore routes to the implementer per the rule, and the verifier deferring the scope decision to the orchestrator was correct humility, not misapplication. Positive findings stand on spot-check: append-only amendment on serialized_per_test_coverage.md is additions-at-EOF-only with frontmatter untouched and references specled_-cpw; ADR cpw disclosure section present; diff introduces no new 'exact' survivors. On disclosure: the IMPLEMENTED comment claimed obligation-2 complete and specifically claimed it grepped spec.triangle.ex for 'exact', but never ran the same sweep on coverage_closure.ex despite referencing that file's per_test proxy (it filed specled_-jjq) — it overstated the sweep's coverage. This does NOT materially reduce confidence in the other completion claims: the verifier independently re-verified them (ADR D1-D5 fidelity, append-only, spec.check-triangulation grep, keyed_by_test_pid, scenario coverage) and I re-confirmed the append-only amendment and the corpus 'exact' sweep, so the re-verify need only confirm the two rewordings land honestly and re-run the corpus sweep — it need not re-run the other obligations.

Bounce reason: Complete obligation 2 by reconciling the two surviving "exact per-test attribution" overclaims. AUDIT-EXPANDED SCOPE AUTHORIZED (so the re-verify pass does not re-flag these files): (1) lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex — the build_v2/2 @doc wording at line 317 ONLY; (2) test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs — the test NAME at line 219 ONLY. Rewrite both to honest, race-bounded language (observed/approximate, never "exact") referencing specled_-cpw (and specled_-jjq for the file-level-proxy reality of the :per_test clause). NO behavior change, NO conditional/data-flow change, NO assertion changes — @doc prose and the test-name string only. All prior clean findings stand; do not re-touch the other five files.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl-1 (iteration 2, post-bounce)
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.8/codex-impl-1/log

Commit: 170c564
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex (@doc build_v2/2 wording), test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs (test name), .spec/realization_hashes.json (hash reconciliation only, disclosed below)
Audit-expanded scope: lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex (@doc build_v2/2 wording) + test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs (test name), per AUDIT: BOUNCE 2026-07-23
Corpus sweep result: grep -riE "exact.{0,30}(per-?test|attribution)" over lib test .spec -> 1 hit, in .spec/decisions/specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage.md: "Adopters who need provably-exact per-test attribution do not yet have a supported path to it" — a correct honest-negation disclosure (same pattern as the ADR/html "not proven exact" phrasing already ruled clean), not an overclaim, and outside the authorized two-file scope. Zero claim-surface survivors.
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --force --warnings-as-errors, mix format --check-formatted, mix test test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs (15 tests, 0 failures)
Final-tier runs: mix spec.check run twice after reconciliation, both exit 0 and stable (findings=1, the same pre-existing INFO-level branch_guard_missing_subject_update finding on lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex present before this change — unrelated to these edits)

Hash reconciliation disclosure: the @doc-only edit shifted line numbers in coverage_closure.ex, which shifted the line/column metadata embedded in the guard clause's dotted-call AST node for build_v2/2's `is_map(index)` guard. `SpecLedEx.Realization.ApiBoundary`'s `strip_meta/1` clears the outer meta on a `{form, meta, args}` node but does not recurse into `form` itself, so that inner metadata (unrelated to any real signature/behavior change) leaks into the api_boundary hash. Confirmed via git-stash A/B compile: with only the doc edit reverted, the computed hash matched the committed baseline exactly (d4952cf0...); with the doc edit applied, it differs (ed197c71...) — args/guards structurally identical apart from line numbers. Reconciled by computing the new hash through the same code path the orchestrator itself uses on a clean commit (`SpecLedEx.Realization.Orchestrator.api_boundary_hashes/2`) and writing it via `SpecLedEx.Realization.HashStore.merge/2` (the tool-managed, atomic, single-entry-preserving API) — not a hand edit of the JSON. Diff is exactly one entry (d4952cf0... -> ed197c71...), hasher_version unchanged (1).

Summary: Reworded the two surviving "exact per-test attribution" overclaims to observed/race-bounded language referencing specled_-cpw and specled_-jjq, and reconciled the resulting api_boundary realization-hash drift (a line-number-metadata artifact, not a real signature change) via the HashStore merge API.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: verify-2
Log: /Users/mzubieta/.local/state/agentic-runs/specled_ex/specled_-155.8/codex-verify-1/log

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (iteration 2): 3 — lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex, test/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure_test.exs, .spec/realization_hashes.json
- Audit-expanded scope honored: yes — the two code files are exactly the bounce-authorized surfaces (coverage_closure.ex build_v2/2 @doc + coverage_closure_test.exs test name); tool-managed hash file is the disclosed third. No unauthorized file. Per step-11 audit-expanded-scope recognition, the two files are IN scope and not re-flagged.
- Code diff is wording-only: confirmed. coverage_closure.ex hunk touches only the @doc bullet prose (lines 314-320); no def/guard/conditional/data-flow change. test hunk changes only the test-name string at line 219; assertion body byte-identical. Read every hunk.

Semantic checks:
- Rewordings honest + cpw-referenced: yes. @doc now reads 'observed, race-bounded per-test attribution (:ok_per_test) — itself currently a file-level proxy rather than true per-test MFA data (specled_-jjq) — from cumulative MFA-level coverage (:ok_aggregate); neither is exact (specled_-cpw)'. References both cpw (race bound) and jjq (proxy). Test name now 'observed per-test attribution is possible only under :ok_per_test' — drops 'exact', honest. No weasel wording implying exactness.
- Corpus sweep (both phrase orders): 2 total hits, both honest disclosure in the aggregate_first ADR, zero overclaims.
  * exact->(per-test|attribution): 1 hit — ADR:257 'provably-exact per-test attribution do not yet have a supported path to' = honest-negation (matches the iteration-2 record's claimed single survivor).
  * (per-test|attribution)->exact: 1 hit — ADR:188 heading 'Per-test attribution is race-bounded, not exact (specled_-cpw)' = disclosure heading, correct.
  Zero claim-surface survivors in lib/test.
- Hash reconciliation: single-entry confirmed — git diff of .spec/realization_hashes.json shows exactly one changed entry, SpecLedEx.Review.CoverageClosure.build_v2/2 (d4952cf0... -> ed197c71...), hasher_version unchanged (1). Signature-identity spot-check: build_v2/2 def line textually identical across commits — 'def build_v2(index, opts \\ []) when is_map(index) do' at 9fb56ef:371 vs HEAD:373 (line-shift only), corroborating the wp7 strip_meta line-metadata-leak causal story. mix compile --force + spec.check x2: both exit 0, status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=1 (pre-existing INFO branch finding on lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex, unrelated), clean working tree after both runs — stable.
- Targeted verification: pass. mix compile --force --warnings-as-errors clean; mix format --check-formatted clean; mix test coverage_closure_test.exs 15/0 (assertions unchanged); mix test html_test.exs 89/0 (label surfaces untouched — regression clean).
- Prior positive findings: not re-run per audit ruling (ADR D1-D5 fidelity, append-only amendment, coherence-pass fixes, iteration-1 scope cleanliness — re-confirmed by auditor).
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design).

Verdict: PASS

Gaps (if FAIL): none.

Notes: The two surviving ADR 'exact' mentions are the intended durable honest-disclosure language (a negation and a 'not exact' heading), correctly outside the two-file bounce scope; leaving them is correct, not a gap.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8 (orchestrator)
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/0a6853df-a850-4f0b-9cf5-09b2923d30b8.jsonl

Reasoning: Iteration-2 VERIFY reported PASS with the bounce remediation fully
delivered: wording-only diff confined to the audit-expanded scope, honest
race-bounded language referencing specled_-cpw/jjq, zero overclaim survivors
in both corpus sweep directions, and single-entry tool-managed hash
reconciliation with the wp7 causal story spot-confirmed. Iteration-1 positive
findings stand per the audit's confidence ruling. No auditor re-spawn needed.

title: T8: Spec + ADR edits (coverage_capture, triangulation, spec_review; new aggregate-first ADR)
